title: National Board of Accountants and Auditors (Examinations and Training) By-laws, 2003
title_en: National Board of Accountants and Auditors (Examinations and Training) By-laws, 2003

provisions.title: Citation
provisions.content: These By-laws may be cited as the National Board of Accountants and Auditors (Examinations and Training) By-laws, 2003.

provisions.title: Interpreta­tion
provisions.content: In these By-laws— " Act " means the Auditors and Accountants (Registration) Act, 1972; " Executive Director " means the Executive Director of the Board; " NBAA " means the National Board of Accountants and Auditors [ Act No. 33 of 1972 ]

provisions.title: Application
provisions.content: (1) Application to sit for any level of NBAA examination shall be made in the form specified in the Schedule to these By-laws. (2) Each application form shall be accompanied by— (a) prescribed fees payable to the Executive Director ; (b) prescribed documents necessary to support the application. (3) In addition to the requirements specified under paragraph (1) , the applicant shall submit— (a) three recently taken identical passport size photographs duly signed at the back; and (b) photocopies of all relevant certificates duly authenticated by a Magistrate, Notary Public, or by the Executive Director . (4) Acceptance to candidacy registration is on satisfaction of meeting the candidacy registration criteria as determined by the Board from time to time. (5) The Board may review the existing minimum entry requirements lor any level of examination as is deemed necessary.

provisions.title: Candidacy registration criteria
provisions.content: Any person applying for candidacy shall— (a) be required to satisfy the Board that he has the qualifications prescribed as the minimum necessary to sit for any particular level of examination; and (b) satisfy the Board that he has completed the' prescribed minimum training period.

provisions.title: Registers
provisions.content: The Board shall have two categories of candidacy registration, namely: (a) the Accounting Technician Examination candidacy registration category, and (b) the Professional Examination candidacy registration categoiy.

provisions.title: Registra­tion nu­mber
provisions.content: (1) Upon admission and registration as a candidate, the Board shall issue the candidate with an identity card showing the candidate's registration number. (2) The candidate's registration number shall be a permanent examination reference with the Board until one ceases to be a candidate.

provisions.title: Cessation of a candi­dacy
provisions.content: A candidate of the Board shall cease to be a candidate upon the occurrence of any of the following— (a) the annual subscription fees being in arrears for more than three years from the date on which the fees became due; (b) on being convicted of a criminal offence; (c) on being declared medically unfit to continue with examinations; (d) on furnishing false information to the Board during the examinations; (e) on failure to sit for examinations for a period of three consecutive years from registration; (f) on being declared bankrupt; (g) on successful completion of programme undertaken, or (h) on death.

provisions.title: Re-admission
provisions.content: A person who ceases to be a candidate in accordance with the provisions of clause 7 may, upon making an application to the Board, be readmitted on such conditions as the Board may specify.

provisions.title: Examination admission letter
provisions.content: A candidate who is registered by the Board to sit for an examination shall be issued with an admission letter specifying— (a) the name of the center at which the candidate shall write the examination; (b) the examination time table; (c) the examination index number; and (d) the examination paper(s) to be attempted.

provisions.title: Examination center
provisions.content: NBAA examinations may be conducted in any center as long as nanon that center has met the minimum number of candidates required.

provisions.title: Conducnting an examination
provisions.content: (1) A candidate registered to sit for the examinations at any center shall be required— (a) to show the identity card and examination admission letter before entering the examination hall; (b) strictly not to carry any unauthorized materials or gadgets other than those specifically permitted by the Board in the examination hall; (c) to sit only at the desk which bears his index number; (d) to write his index number only on the indicated space of the examination cover; (e) to observe any general instructions that may be given by the invigilator and those instructions appearing at the top of the examination question paper; (f) not to leave the examination hall until thirty minutes have elapsed after the commencement of the examination and the candidate having signed the attendance sheet against his index number; (g) not to engage in any unprofessional conduct aimed at assisting him during the examination; (h) to maintain an examination atmosphere by not engaging in any conversation or behaviour that may distract the attention of any other candidate; (i) not to smoke or eat in the examination hall; (j) not to leave the examination hall before the examination ends without the permission of the invigilator and when necessary to do so, he shall be accompanied by an assistant invigilator; (k) not to take restricted materials or examination scripts from the examination hall; and (l) to stop writing immediately after the invigilator declares the examination is over. (2) A candidate who is guilty of dishonesty in connection with the examination or infringes any of the provision in paragraph (1) of these by-laws is liable to disciplinary action.

provisions.title: Disciplinary action
provisions.content: (1) The Education and Publications Committee shall recommend to the Governing Board all matters concerning action irregularities in any of the Board's examinations. Disciplinary procedures for misconduct (2) Every candidate of the Board is required to comply with the provisions of these By-laws. (3) Disciplinary action for breach of the provisions of these By-laws shall include any of the following: (a) a written warning; (b) a partial nullification of the results of a particular subject; (c) impose a fine, not exceeding one hundred thousand which shall be payable to the Board; (d) nullification of all examination results; and (e) nullification of all examination results together with the restriction from sitting for the Board's examinations for a specified period as may be determined by the Governing Board from time to time; (f) barring the candidate from sitting the Board's examinations.

provisions.title: Appeals
provisions.content: (1) The Governing Board shall have powers to decide on any matter concerning examinations of the Board. (2) The decision of the Governing Board shall be subjected to review upon application to a court of law.

provisions.title: Pass grade
provisions.content: A candidate who obtains grades A, B or C in all subjects shall be deemed to have passed that particular examination level.

provisions.title: Fail grade
provisions.content: A candidate who obtains marks below grade C in more than one subject shall be deemed to have failed that particular examination level.

provisions.title: Referred candidate
provisions.content: (1) A candidate shall be deemed to be referred in the subject if candidate he fails one subject in any examinati on level. (2) A referred candidate shall— (a) be required to clear the referred paper within a prescribed time frame starting from the date of announcement of the examination results which the referral was obtained; (b) loose his referral status upon expiry of the prescribed time frame as may be determined by the Board.

provisions.title: Exemption criteria
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall grant exemption to qualifications obtained within and outside the country which are deemed to be equivalent to the examination levels or papers offered by the Board. (2) A qualification shall be honoured for exemption purposes within five years after being acquired. (3) An exempted candidate shall be deemed to have passed or failed in any level of examination in non-exempted subject(s) upon acquiring a pass or fails grade respectively in that examination level.

provisions.title: Declara­tion of examina­tion results
provisions.content: (1) The Education and Publications Committee or the Committee entrusted with the Board's examination matters shall approve the examination results for ratification by the Governing Board. (2) The Governing Board shall declare the examination results when it is ready to do so and such results shall be communicated to the candidates immediately after the approval of the Governing Board. (3) No candidate shall be informed of the numerical grade secured in any paper. (4) No candidate shall be entertained with enquiries as to when the examination results would be declared.

provisions.title: Retention of examina­tion scripts and rela­ted docu­ment
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall retain the examination scripts and other examination documents for a period of two years, after which they will be disposed off in a manner as shall be determined by the Board. (2) In case such documents are involved in any dispute, then such documents shall be retained until such time when the dispute shall have been resolved.

provisions.title: Testing the newly intro­duced Statutes/ Laws
provisions.content: The Board shall be observing a six months period before examining newly introduced statutes.

provisions.title: Awards
provisions.content: (1) A candidate who successfully completed an examination at any level shall be awarded by the Board relevant certificates as follows: (a) an Accounting Technician shall be issued with: (i) an academic transcript immediately after successfully completing the examinations, showing the candidates' examination performance for the examinations attempted and passed; (ii) an Accounting Technician Certificate (ATEC) on successful completion of a one-year practical experience and on meeting the membership registration requirements; (b) a Certified Public Accountant shall be issued with: (i) an academic transcript immediately after successfully completing the examinations, showing the candidates' examination performance for the examinations attempted and passed; (ii) an Associate Certified Public Accountant in Public Practice Certificate (ACPA-PP(T) or an Associate Certified Public Accountant Certificate (ACP A {T}) as the case may be on successful completion of a three (3) year's practical experience and on meeting the membership registration requirements. (2) A candidate who successfully completes intermediate stages of either the Accounting Technician Certificate (ATEC) or Certified Public Accountant Certificate (CPA) programme shall be awarded STATEMENT OF RESULTS for the level passed.

provisions.title: Training
provisions.content: (1) The Board shall monitor both public and private institutions which train candidates to sit for any of the Board's examinations. (2) The Board's candidates shall— (a) attend part time courses in institutions recognized by the Board for a prescribed training period; (b) attend full time courses at accredited institutions which may result into exemption of parts of the NBAA examinations.

provisions.title: Admissi­on require­ment
provisions.content: (1) Upon admission, a candidate shall be required to pay the following fees— (a) registration fees; and (b) examination fees, as may be prescribed by the Board from time to time. (2) The prescribed annual subscription fees shall be paid upon admission and thereafter on the first day of January of each year and shall cease to be payable on the candidate's successful completion of the Board's examinations.

provisions.title: Non refundable examination fee
provisions.content: (1) Examination fees paid in pursuant to Clause 23 (l)(b) shall not be carried forward to the next examination session unless— (a) there is a written withdrawal by the candidate from sitting for the examination on or before the closing date in which case, the total amount paid shall be carried forward; or (b) there is a written withdrawal by the candidate on medical grounds supported by an acceptable medical report, in which case, half the amount paid may be carried forward, as the Board may determine; or (c) the examination fee to be carried forward shall be valid up to the next examination session only.

provisions.title: Revocation
provisions.content: The National Board of Accountants and Auditors (Examinations and Training) By-laws, 2000 are hereby revoked. Schedule (Under By-law 3 (1)) Forms [Please note: Forms have not been reproduced.
